data.title: NIBE Industrier AB (publ) reports Q1 results
data.description:
• NIBE Industrier AB (publ) press release (OTCPK:NDRBF): Q1 EARNINGS per share adjusted for items affecting comparability before and after dilution, based on the average number of shares outstanding during the period, amounted to SEK 0.19 (0.02)
  • SALES amounted to SEK 9,673 (9,494 million)

data.title: Stock index futures fall as investors digest retail sales, PPI data
data.description:
Stock index futures fell on Thursday as investors digested a slew of macroeconomic data, including retail sales and wholesale inflation reports.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.4%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.5%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.51%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 4 basis points to 4.01%.

Headline producer prices unexpectedly fell on a monthly basis in April. Retail sales rose just 0.1% in April.

The Philly Fed Manufacturing Index rose to -4.0 in May, vs. -10.0 consensus, from -26.4 in the prior month.

Initial jobless claims for the week ended May 10 were 229 K vs. 229K consensus and 229K prior (revised from 228K).

Wall Street on Wednesday eked out a gain, extending a two-day rally and moving further into positive territory for the year.

"The strong recent equity rally showed some signs of exhaustion over the past 24 hours, even though gains for the tech mega caps helped the S&P 500 extend its gains since the closing low on April 7 to +18.26%," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

Donald Trump’s Middle East tour continued to generate headlines yesterday, but their market impact was narrower in comparison to Tuesday’s rally, Sidorov added.

Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell will give a speech at the Thomas Laubach Research Conference concerning Framework Review.

data.title: ZJK Industrial secures patent in China for inspection-control system
data.description:
• ZJK Industrial (NASDAQ:ZJK) has patented an innovative image recognition-based inspection-control system for carrier tape packaging.
  • Traditional SMT carrier tape inspections rely on manual checks post-packaging, a slow and labor-intensive process. ZJK’s innovation replaces this with automated, in-line detection, boosting efficiency and accuracy.
  • The patent, granted by the China National Intellectual Property Administration, covers a surface-mount technology carrier tape packaging inspection-control system based on image recognition.
  • "Meanwhile, the industry-wide shift toward automation and intelligent systems continues to drive demand for SMT packaging products and cutting-edge inspection equipment, both of which remain integral to modern manufacturing and are evolving alongside technological advancements." said Ning Ding, CEO of ZJK.

data.title: Headline producer prices unexpectedly fall on monthly basis in April
data.description:
The Producer Price Index unexpectedly fell 0.5% M/M in April, compared with the +0.2% expected and 0.0% in March (revised from -0.4%), according to data released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on Thursday.

That translates to a Y/Y gain of 2.4%, as compared to the consensus of +2.5% and +3.4% prior (revised from +3.2%).

The April decline in the index for final demand is mainly due to prices for final demand services, which decreased 0.7%. The index for final demand goods was unchanged.

Over 40% of the April decline in the index for final demand services is primarily due to margins for machinery and vehicle wholesaling, which fell 6.1%.

Stripping out volatile food and energy costs, core PPI fell 0.4% M/M, as compared to +0.3% consensus, and +0.4% prior (revised from -0.1%)

On a yearly basis, it translated to +3.1% meeting consensus, as compared to +4.0% (revised from +3.3%) 

Prices for residential electric power, fresh and dry vegetables, non- electronic cigarettes, and utility natural gas also moved up. Conversely, the index for chicken eggs dropped 39.4%. Prices for gasoline, gas fuels, diesel fuel, and primary basic organic chemicals also fell,  BLS said.

Prices for final demand less food, energy, and trade services edged down 0.1% in April, the first decline since falling 0.8% in April 2020. 

On Tuesday, the April Consumer Price Index rose 0.2% in April, better than the +0.3% consensus, but rising from -0.1% in March, according to data released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

data.title: OneMeta signs licensing agreement with Invictus BPO
data.description:
• OneMeta (OTCQB:ONEI) has signed a licensing agreement and strategic collaboration with Invictus.
  • Under this agreement, Invictus will integrate OneMeta's VerbumAgentis and VerbumSDK technologies into its services and technology platform iKunnect, empowering contact centers and other businesses to deliver enhanced customer experiences through unparalleled AI-based multilingual capabilities.
  • ONEI closed -4.54% at $0.229.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Hexagon Composites reports Q1 results
data.description:

  • Revenue of NOK 912M, vs. NOK 945M a year ago

data.title: Philly Fed rose to -4.0 in May
data.description:
The Philly Fed Manufacturing Index rose to -4.0 in May, vs. -10.0 consensus, from -26.4 in the prior month.

The diffusion index for current general activity rose from -26.4 to -4.0 in May, paring some of last month’s sharp decline but remaining negative.

The new orders index rose 42 points to 7.5, offsetting nearly all of April’s decline.

The shipments index declined for the fourth consecutive month, falling 4 points to -13.0, its lowest reading since November 2023.

The firms reported overall increases in employment, and the employment index rose 16 points to 16.5 in May.

Most firms continued to report no change in employment levels (68 percent), while the share of firms reporting increases (23 percent) exceeded the share reporting decreases (7 percent).

The average workweek index rose from -12.7 to 2.0.The diffusion index for future general activity jumped 40 points to 47.2 in May after two months of low readings.

data.title: Vallourec S.A. reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Vallourec S.A. press release (OTCPK:VLOUF): Q1 EBITDA of €207 million, with strong 21% EBITDA margin
  • Revenues of €991M (+1% Y/Y)

  • Second Quarter 2025 Group EBITDA is expected to range between €170 million and €200 million:

    • In Tubes, EBITDA per tonne is expected to be flat to slightly higher sequentially, while volumes are anticipated to be flat to slightly down sequentially.
    • In Mine & Forest, production sold is expected to be around 1.5 million tonnes. Profitability will be determined by prevailing iron ore market prices.

    Full Year 2025 Group EBITDA is expected to reflect a second half improvement:

    • In Tubes, international shipments are expected to increase in H2 2025 compared to H1 2025 due to strong bookings over recent quarters. EBITDA per tonne should improve in H2 2025 compared to H1 2025 especially due to higher invoiced international prices and cost savings.
    • In Mine & Forest, production sold is expected to be around 6 million tonnes. Profitability will be determined by prevailing iron ore market prices.

data.title: Allstate estimates catastrophe losses for April of $594M
data.description:
• Property and casualty insurer Allstate Corporation (NYSE:ALL) on Thursday announced estimated catastrophe losses for the month of April of $594 million or $469 million, after-tax.
  • Catastrophe losses for April include 11 events, with approximately 60% of the losses related to two geographically widespread wind and hail events.
  • Source: Press Release
